content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach responds purely to content throughout, never naming how the client is showing up. The client offers multiple readable signals — the linguistic caveat 'just me' in turn 1, the deflection to partner's busy week in turn 3, the admission of guilt in turn 7, and the flat, defeated 'Maybe. I don't know' in turn 9 — and the coach acknowledges none of them. There are no observation statements, no somatic or energetic tracking, no naming of the inherited 'selfish' label, no noticing that the client keeps retreating from their own want. Every coach turn is advice or reassurance pointed at the topic, not the speaker.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Verdict-giving with zero read; misses the linguistic caveat 'just me' and the framing of the question as a request for permission."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Engages partner-logistics content; misses that client just deflected away from their own want."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Prescribes scripts; misses the avoidance signal in 'I haven't brought it up yet.'"},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Reframes guilt intellectually rather than noticing or naming the guilt as it is showing up in the body or voice."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Takes 'Maybe. I don't know' at face value as something to overcome with encouragement, missing the obvious deflation/withdrawal read."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["linguistic-caveat", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "notes": "Coach also walks straight into the spiritual-narcissism and skip-to-compassion traps, but those are intervention-quality issues; here the diagnostic is that no read is ever made visible."
}
